jokes.text: Yes, Ross, we know they have magazines with pictures of naked women in them.
jokes.setup: Ross starts to tell news with 'You're not gonna believe this'
jokes.punchline: Chandler assumes it's about discovering porn magazines
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm with good misdirection. Sets up the porn magazine theme while deflating Ross's excitement.

jokes.text: Don't tell him about the videos!
jokes.setup: Following Chandler's porn magazine comment
jokes.punchline: Joey escalates to videos, staying in character as naive
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation joke that fits Joey's character. Functional but not remarkable.

jokes.text: They print jokes, interviews, hard-hitting journalism. It's not just about the pictures.
jokes.setup: Monica's surprise that Playboy prints jokes
jokes.punchline: Ross defensively lists Playboy's 'legitimate' content
jokes.explanation: Classic 'I read it for the articles' joke. Predictable but fits Ross's academic pretensions.

jokes.text: Didn't work on Mom, it's not gonna work on us.
jokes.setup: Ross's defense of Playboy's intellectual content
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals Ross has used this excuse before on their mother
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that adds backstory and character depth. Perfect economy and timing.

jokes.text: It was also funny when I made it up.
jokes.setup: Monica compliments the joke's funniness
jokes.punchline: Chandler claims he created it, setting up the episode's central conflict
jokes.explanation: Clean setup for the episode's main plot with good sarcastic delivery typical of Chandler.

jokes.text: Hey, tell Dan thanks.
jokes.setup: Chandler mentions telling Dan the joke
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically thanks someone who heard 'his' joke secondhand
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback showing Ross's frustration. Economical but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: I was just reading the joke below it. Man, that one is funny.
jokes.setup: Joey appears to be commenting on the disputed joke
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's actually reading a different joke entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - appears engaged but is actually distracted. Great misdirection and character work.

jokes.text: You tell a lot of jokes.
jokes.setup: Ross asks Monica to remember him telling the joke
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt explanation for why she can't remember
jokes.explanation: Monica's practical, slightly cutting response. Good economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: You know there's naked chicks in there, right?
jokes.setup: Ross and Chandler arguing intensely over the joke
jokes.punchline: Joey reminds them of the magazine's obvious main attraction
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey priorities. Good contrast to the intellectual argument, very in character.

jokes.text: I don't want to miss when they were skinny.
jokes.setup: Joey wants to watch Heart documentary at his place
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's interested in seeing the band members when they were thinner
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny observation about celebrity documentaries. Good Joey superficiality.

jokes.text: I tell you, the music these kids listen to today... it's a lot of noise to me.
jokes.setup: Joey's cable is out, he's watching basic TV
jokes.punchline: Joey suddenly sounds like an old man complaining about modern music
jokes.explanation: Funny character moment showing Joey adapting to circumstances. Good age-inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: I know things have been tight since Janine moved out. God, was she hot.
jokes.setup: Chandler offers to loan Joey money for bills
jokes.punchline: Both get distracted remembering Joey's attractive ex-roommate
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows both characters getting distracted by attractive women.

jokes.text: Paid your phone bill? -No.
jokes.setup: Joey's financial troubles with cable
jokes.punchline: Reveals his financial problems are even worse than expected
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation of Joey's financial woes. Economical but predictable.

jokes.text: Yikes!
jokes.setup: Phoebe wants to look at Playboy with Joey
jokes.punchline: Her shocked reaction to whatever she sees
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction to implied visual content. Functional but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Sure. Like in the case of this young woman... she has lost her clothes. So she rides naked on the horse, she's crying out, 'Where are they?'
jokes.setup: Joey asks if Playboy pictures tell stories
jokes.punchline: Phoebe creates an innocent, childlike narrative for a sexy photo
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe moment turning sexual imagery into innocent storytelling. Perfect character integration and originality.

jokes.text: She's not gonna find them, lying in the grass like that.
jokes.setup: Continuing her innocent interpretation of the photo
jokes.punchline: Phoebe offers practical advice to the model about finding her clothes
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up maintaining Phoebe's innocent worldview. Great escalation of the absurd premise.

jokes.text: Remember the days when you'd go out to the barn... Lift up your shirt and bend over?
jokes.setup: Looking at Playboy photos with Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Joey inappropriately shares what he assumes is a universal experience
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable Joey overshare that's very in character. Creates cringe humor effectively.

jokes.text: She's cute, she's outdoorsy. And she knows how to build a fire. That'll come in handy.
jokes.setup: Joey evaluating a Playboy model for dating potential
jokes.punchline: He focuses on practical survival skills rather than obvious attributes
jokes.explanation: Funny Joey logic prioritizing practical skills. Good subversion of expected male gaze.

jokes.text: Rachel. What? I don't know. Me neither.
jokes.setup: Joey asks Phoebe which friend she'd date
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately says Rachel, then both try to backtrack awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment setting up later conflict. Shows Phoebe's honesty getting her in trouble.

jokes.text: I know I cracked up, but I'm not even sure I got it. The doctor is a monkey. And monkeys can't write out prescriptions.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the disputed joke
jokes.punchline: Phoebe admits she didn't understand the joke and explains it literally
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about the quality of the disputed joke. Phoebe's literal explanation undermines its humor.

jokes.text: You are not allowed to laugh at my joke.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining why she didn't understand the joke
jokes.punchline: Ross's possessive reaction to someone not getting 'his' joke
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's competitiveness and need for credit.

jokes.text: Well, I think the Hef would disagree. Which is why he sent me a check... for $100.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming ownership of the joke
jokes.punchline: Chandler reveals he actually got paid for it, escalating the stakes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that raises the stakes. 'The Hef' is a nice casual reference to Hugh Hefner.

jokes.text: I was gonna stick it in the ATM. But now I think I'll show the sexy teller... that I am a published writer.
jokes.setup: Chandler has a $100 check from Playboy
jokes.punchline: His plan to impress a bank teller with his 'literary' achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler moment - using dubious achievement to impress women. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I've had to cut down on some luxuries like... paying for stuff, so...
jokes.setup: Joey explaining why he can't pay for coffee
jokes.punchline: Calling paying for things a 'luxury' rather than necessity
jokes.explanation: Great Joey logic turning basic responsibility into optional luxury. Good economy and character work.

jokes.text: See, I was a regular on a soap opera, you know? And to go from that to this, it's just...
jokes.setup: Gunther offers Joey a job at Central Perk
jokes.punchline: Joey's pride about his career trajectory from soap star to coffee shop worker
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's misplaced pride about his 'career.' Relatable actor struggle.

jokes.text: Plus, you get to stare at Rachel as much as you want. What? Flexible hours.
jokes.setup: Gunther listing job benefits for Joey
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals his own creepy obsession with Rachel, then covers awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gunther moment - his obsession slipping out then awkward cover. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: I'm gonna get a joke journal, you know? And document the date and time of every single one of my jokes.
jokes.setup: Chandler frustrated about joke theft
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top solution of cataloging all his jokes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Chandler's obsessive tendencies. Funny overreaction to the situation.

jokes.text: Maybe it's the sound of Ross climbing into my brain to steal my thoughts.
jokes.setup: They hear a sound from the living room
jokes.punchline: Chandler's paranoid explanation involving brain theft
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist escalation of Chandler's paranoia. Good imagery and economy.

jokes.text: Just trying to re-create some of the fun that we had at my place. Remember? When you picked Rachel over me?
jokes.setup: Monica commenting on Phoebe and Rachel looking cute together
jokes.punchline: Reveals her passive-aggressive hurt about not being chosen
jokes.explanation: Good Monica passive-aggression. Perfect callback with character-appropriate bitterness.

jokes.text: The reason I was leaning a little bit more toward Rachel than you... is just, you know, you're... high maintenance. Let's go to lunch!
jokes.setup: Monica demanding to know why she wasn't chosen
jokes.punchline: Phoebe blurts out the harsh truth then immediately tries to escape
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe honesty followed by immediate regret. The abrupt topic change is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: You write out a list, and we'll go through it point by point. No, you're right, you're easygoing.
jokes.setup: Monica demanding proof she's not high maintenance
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately backtracks rather than face Monica's intensity
jokes.explanation: Monica proving she's high maintenance by demanding detailed proof she's not. Great character irony.

jokes.text: Oh, okay. You're not a pushover.
jokes.setup: Rachel insisting she's not a pushover
jokes.punchline: Monica's obviously sarcastic agreement
jokes.explanation: Simple sarcastic reaction. Economical but predictable sister dynamic.

jokes.text: You're not invited to lunch! What do you think? Pretty strong, I think.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to prove she's not a pushover
jokes.punchline: Her mild 'assertion' followed by seeking approval for it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rachel moment - trying to be assertive but seeking validation. Good character irony.

jokes.text: Oh, I love that Japanese place. -I'm sick of Japanese. Not there. Wherever you want to go is cool.
jokes.setup: Rachel claiming to be strong and not a pushover
jokes.punchline: Immediately caves when Monica rejects her restaurant suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction of Rachel's claims. Excellent structure showing rather than telling.

jokes.text: I don't see you asking other paying customers to put on aprons.
jokes.setup: Gunther telling Joey to put on his apron
jokes.punchline: Joey's logic that he should be treated like a regular customer
jokes.explanation: Good Joey logic trying to have it both ways - employee benefits without employee responsibilities.

jokes.text: It's supposed to go the other direction. So's your apron. You're wearing it like a cape.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining his career trajectory from actor to waiter
jokes.punchline: Chandler's comment about the apron being worn incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Great Chandler one-liner with visual comedy element. Perfect economy and good callback structure.

jokes.text: Don't eat yellow snow.
jokes.setup: Chandler giving Joey his 'first tip' as a waiter
jokes.punchline: Old joke advice instead of actual service tip
jokes.explanation: Predictable dad joke. Fits Chandler but relies on familiar material rather than original wit.

jokes.text: And if anyone is ever rude to you, sneeze muffin.
jokes.setup: Rachel giving Joey advice about customer service
jokes.punchline: Suggests food contamination as revenge for rude customers
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly dark advice from sweet Rachel. Good subversion of expectations.

jokes.text: How about clearing out so I can get some new customers? It's all about turnover.
jokes.setup: Joey being nice to his friends as customers
jokes.punchline: Immediately kicks them out for business reasons on his first day
jokes.explanation: Good Joey enthusiasm for his new job overriding friendship. Character-appropriate business naivety.

jokes.text: That's true, I am flaky. So, what, you're just okay with being flaky? Yeah, totally.
jokes.setup: Monica and Rachel calling Phoebe flaky as criticism
jokes.punchline: Phoebe completely accepts the label without defensiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe self-awareness and acceptance. Great contrast to Monica and Rachel's defensiveness.

jokes.text: I'm flaky. I'll say anything.
jokes.setup: Monica and Rachel getting upset about being called high maintenance and pushover
jokes.punchline: Phoebe dismisses her own previous statements due to being flaky
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke using Phoebe's character trait to undermine the entire conflict. Perfect economy and logic.

jokes.text: I'm the lead guy's best friend. I wait for him and save his seat. Listen. 'I'm sorry, that seat's saved.'
jokes.setup: Joey describing his audition for a 'great part'
jokes.punchline: The entire role is just one line about saving a seat
jokes.explanation: Good commentary on small acting parts. Shows Joey's optimism about minimal roles.

jokes.text: That's the whole part? -Maybe he's not his best friend.
jokes.setup: Joey's enthusiasm for his one-line part
jokes.punchline: Gunther's dry observation about the role's importance
jokes.explanation: Good Gunther deadpan moment. Perfect economy and logical observation.

jokes.text: The coffeehouse will be closed for about an hour. It's for the kids. Yeah, to keep the kids off drugs. It's a very important issue in this month's Playboy.
jokes.setup: Chandler needs to clear out Central Perk
jokes.punchline: Creates increasingly ridiculous cover story involving Playboy's anti-drug campaign
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation combining the episode's themes. Chandler's quick thinking on display.

jokes.text: But I'm your brother. We're family. That's the most important thing in the world. Don't try to sway her. I'm your only chance to have a baby.
jokes.setup: Ross and Chandler trying to influence Monica's decision
jokes.punchline: Ross's emotional manipulation followed by Chandler's darker counter-manipulation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from sentiment to manipulation. Dark but perfectly in character for both.

jokes.text: See, I would never snap at you like that. Two babies.
jokes.setup: Chandler snapping at Monica during his explanation
jokes.punchline: Ross pointing out the inconsistency, Chandler immediately upping his baby offer
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and economy. Chandler's desperation leading to absurd bidding war.

jokes.text: I'm Dr. Monkey!
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his connection to monkeys through his career and Marcel
jokes.punchline: His triumphant declaration of joke ownership
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's academic pride. Callback to his profession and Marcel.

jokes.text: You are both idiots. It's not funny. And it's offensive to women. And doctors. And monkeys.
jokes.setup: Monica asked to judge between Ross and Chandler's joke claims
jokes.punchline: Declares the joke terrible and offensive to multiple groups
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica practical response cutting through the nonsense. Great meta-commentary on the joke's quality.

jokes.text: Wow. You know what? That is the best fake speech I think I've ever heard. Really? I've heard better.
jokes.setup: Monica praising Chandler's rehearsed speech about her
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual dismissal of Monica's praise
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe deflation of Monica's moment. Perfectly casual delivery undermines the sentiment.

jokes.text: They say you're high maintenance... but it's okay, because I like... maintaining you.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to make Monica feel better about being high maintenance
jokes.punchline: His sweet but awkward wordplay about 'maintaining' her
jokes.explanation: Sweet Chandler moment with clever wordplay. Perfectly captures their relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: You know, suddenly I find you very attractive.
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying she's okay not being Monica's girlfriend choice
jokes.punchline: Rachel's mock jealousy over Phoebe's confidence
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the girlfriend selection topic. Shows Rachel's appreciation for confidence.

jokes.text: Okay.
jokes.setup: Rachel demanding Joey get his job back
jokes.punchline: Gunther's immediate, casual agreement without argument
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gunther moment - his crush on Rachel making him instantly compliant. Great economy.

jokes.text: Pretty nice, huh? Now who's a pushover? Rach, you're in my seat.
jokes.setup: Rachel proud of standing up to Gunther
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives up her seat when asked
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction of Rachel's claim. Excellent structure showing vs. telling character traits.

jokes.text: Joey. No way, I'm not answering that.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking who they'd pick between Monica and Rachel
jokes.punchline: Joey blurts out an answer then Chandler refuses to play the game
jokes.explanation: Good callback ending. Joey's honesty vs. Chandler's diplomacy. Solid character consistency.
